title: Lasianthera secundiflora
intro: Lasianthera secundiflora, also known as the African tea tree, is a plant species native to the savannas and grasslands of tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Lasianthera secundiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lasianthera secundiflora is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the formulation of topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
